{"step_by_step_thinking": "Metoprolol is a beta-blocker, and verapamil is a non-dihydropyridine calcium channel blocker. Both of these drugs have negative chronotropic and inotropic effects. When combined, they can lead to bradycardia and possible AV block due to their additive effects on heart rate and conduction. Atrial fibrillation, torsades de pointes, and tachycardia are not the expected outcomes of combining these two drugs.", "answer_choice": "B"}
